title: Acoustics and Music Theory — ELC Costs (SuperBEST v5)

superBEST_v5_costs.note: sin/cos are OUTSIDE real ELC(ℝ) by T01 (infinitely many zeros)

equations.name: Frequency ratio (musical intervals)
equations.notes: Simplest acoustic ratio. Basis of all interval arithmetic.

equations.name: Equal temperament tuning
equations.notes: pow(2, n/12) is NOT used directly because the exponent n/12 is irrational in general; instead rewrite as exp(mul(div(n,12), ln(2))). ln(2) is a real constant treated as 0n. The div→mul→exp chain costs 4n; the final mul with f₀ adds 2n.

equations.name: Beat frequency
decomposition: abs(x): in real ELC, abs(x) = sqrt(x²) requires ln(x) which is undefined for x < 0
For f₁ > f₂ (physical assumption): sub alone = 2n

abs_flag.problem: abs(x) is not representable in real ELC for arbitrary signed reals. The identity abs(x) = sqrt(x²) = exp(ln(x²)/2) = exp(mul(2, ln(x))/2) requires ln(x), which is undefined (or complex) when x ≤ 0.
abs_flag.connection_to_X7: This is the same boundary encountered in X7: sign-sensitive functions create obstructions in real ELC. The absolute value is the prototype of a piecewise-smooth function that requires case splitting — not an ELC composition.
abs_flag.conclusion: abs is OUTSIDE real ELC(ℝ) for general signed inputs. Beat frequency is INSIDE only when the sign of f₁−f₂ is known a priori.

equations.name: Decibel scale
equations.notes: log₁₀ is not a primitive; it is computed as ln(·)/ln(10). Both ln(10) and the scalar 20 are constants (0n each). Requires A, A₀ > 0, which is physically guaranteed for acoustic amplitudes.

equations.name: Resonant frequency (LC circuit)
equations.notes: L, C > 0 physically. 2π is a fixed constant. recip used instead of div because numerator is 1.

equations.name: Sound intensity (point source)
decomposition: pow(r, 2) = 3n  [r > 0 physically, so pow is valid]
equations.notes: r is a physical radius so r > 0; pow(r, 2) costs 3n (not mul) because pow is the general real power operation. r² could also be computed as mul(r, r) = 2n, saving 1n if preferred — but pow is the canonical form.

equations.name: Sound intensity — alternative r² via mul
equations.formula: I = P / (4π r²), with r² = r·r
decomposition: mul(r, r) = 2n  [valid since r is a specific real, not a general power]
equations.notes: If r² is treated as mul(r,r) rather than pow(r,2), cost drops to 5n. This is valid because squaring is a special case of multiplication. Shows that canonical form choice affects cost.

equations.name: Doppler effect
equations.notes: All operations are add/div/mul. No transcendental functions. v + v_s ≠ 0 required (no sonic boom singularity), which is a physical constraint, not an ELC constraint.

equations.name: Reverberation time (Sabine formula)
equations.notes: 0.161 is an empirical constant (= 24·ln(10)/c, c ≈ 343 m/s), treated as a fixed real (0n). V is room volume, A is total absorption — both positive reals. Simplest multi-variable acoustic formula in this set.

equations.name: Wave equation solution (sinusoidal wave)
decomposition: sin(θ) = OUTSIDE real ELC (T01: sin has infinitely many zeros, cannot be represented in ELC(ℝ))
In complex ELC: sin(θ) = Im(exp(i·θ)), computable via exp(i·θ) = 1n in ELC(ℂ)
equations.elc_cost: OUTSIDE real ELC(ℝ) / INSIDE complex ELC(ℂ)
equations.notes: The obstruction is sin, not the linear argument kx−ωt. The argument is 6n in ELC(ℝ) but sin applied to it is not in ELC(ℝ). In ELC(ℂ), sin is representable via the imaginary part of exp.

key_finding.title: Spectral/Temporal Boundary in Acoustics
key_finding.statement: Acoustic SPECTRUM (frequency content and energy distribution) is INSIDE real ELC(ℝ). Acoustic WAVEFORM (time-domain wave values) is OUTSIDE real ELC(ℝ).
outside_real_ELC: Any function with sin or cos of a varying argument
abs(x) for general signed inputs (not a waveform, but sign-sensitive — same obstruction class)
key_finding.interpretation: The boundary is clean: quantities that describe the frequency domain (ratios, levels, intervals, decay times, cutoffs) are all computable in ELC(ℝ). Quantities that describe the time domain (actual wave displacement as a function of position and time) require sin/cos, which are provably outside ELC(ℝ) by T01. This makes ELC(ℝ) a natural algebra for acoustic engineering (filter design, tuning, level calculations) but not for direct waveform synthesis.
key_finding.complex_ELC_bridge: In ELC(ℂ), sin and cos are representable as imaginary/real parts of exp(i·θ), so complex ELC closes the temporal gap. The spectral/temporal distinction collapses in ELC(ℂ) — it is a purely real phenomenon.
key_finding.abs_note: abs(x) is outside real ELC for general signed reals (same obstruction class as sign-sensitive functions in X7). Beat frequency is inside ELC only under the physical assumption f₁ > f₂.
